zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert於1356年出生在威爾士蒙姆爾時，他的父親Gwilym ap Jenkin29歲，母親Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan21歲。他於1392年與Maud de Morley在英國威爾斯蒙姆爾拉格藍結婚。他們至少是11個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1438年7月4日在威爾士蒙姆爾拉格藍享年82歲，埋葬在威爾士蒙姆爾利安桑特福拉德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert于1356年出生在威尔士蒙姆爾时，他的父亲Gwilym ap Jenkin29岁，母亲Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan21岁。他于1392年与Maud de Morley在英国威尔斯蒙姆爾拉格藍结婚。他们至少是11个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1438年7月4日在威尔士蒙姆爾拉格藍享年82岁，埋葬在威尔士蒙姆爾利安桑特福拉德。

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English, and French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In Britain, this Old French name, introduced by the Normans, reinforced the less common Old English cognate Herebeorht. The surname was taken to Ireland after the Anglo-Norman invasion and in the 16th century.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the German personal name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Harbison
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name Herbert (the change from -er- to -ar- was a common one in Old French and Middle English). An intrusive n later gave Harbinson .

variants.name: Hebard
meanings: English (Surrey):
variant of Herbert , with assimilation of the first r. Compare Hebb .
variant of   Hibbert  .

variants.name: Harbert
meanings: German and English: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbers
meanings: German and Dutch: patronymic from   Herbert  . It is found mainly in Lower Saxony and across the Dutch border.

variants.name: Dickherber
meanings: Probably a nickname for a fat person by the name of Herbert , of which Herber is a short form, or, more likely, in the north from Die(c)kherber, a topographic and distinguishing name for a Herber living by the dike, see Dicke 2. Compare Dickmeyer .

variants.name: Hebert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Pierre, Lucien, Michel, Gaston, Alcide, Camille.
French (Hébert): assimilated form of Herbert . Compare Abair , Abar , Abare , Abear , Aber , Eber , and Ebert .
German: variant of   Heber   1.

variants.name: Herber
meanings: German and Dutch: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbertson
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name   Herbert  .

variants.name: Hebb
meanings: English (East Yorkshire and northeast Midlands): from the Middle English personal name Hebb, Ebb, pet forms of Herbert .

variants.name: Herb
meanings: South German: nickname for a harsh or rough person, from Middle High German herb ‘tart, harsh’.
German: from the personal name Herpo, a pet form of Herbert .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Guillem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert was born in 1356, in Monmouthshire, Wales, his father, Gwilym ap Jenkin, was 29 and his mother, Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan, was 21. He married Maud de Morley in 1392, in Raglan, Monmouthshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 11 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 4 July 1438, in Raglan, Monmouthshire, Wales, at the age of 82, and was buried in Llansantffraed, Monmouthshire, Wales.

Il a épousé Maud de Morley en 1392, à Raglan, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 11 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 4 juillet 1438, à Raglan, Monmouthshire, Galles, à l’âge de 82 ans, et a été enterré à Llansantffraed, Monmouthshire, Galles.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbertが1356年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアで生まれた時，父Gwilym ap Jenkinは29歳，母Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychanは21歳であった。1392年にイギリスウェールズモンモウスシュアイアラグランでMaud de Morleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも11人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1438年7月4日にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアラグランで82歳で死去し，ウェールズモンモウスシュアイアランサントフラエドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert(이)가 1356년 웨일스 몬모우스실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gwilym ap Jenkin(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1392년 영국 웨일스 몬모우스실르 라글런에서 Maud de Morley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1438년 7월 4일 웨일스 몬모우스실르 라글런에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스 몬모우스실르 랜산트프라이드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert родился в 1356 г. в местности монмоутшир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Gwilym ap Jenkin было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud de Morley в 1392 г. в местности раглан, монмоутшир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 11 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 4 июля 1438 г. в местности раглан, монмоутшир, Уэльс в возрасте 82 лет и был похоронен в местности ллансантффрэед, монмоутшир, Уэльс.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert nació en 1356, en Monmouthshire, Gales, su papá, Gwilym ap Jenkin, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Gwenllian verch Hywel Fychan, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Maud de Morley en 1392, en Raglan, Monmouthshire, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 11 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 4 de julio de 1438, en Raglan, Monmouthshire, Gales, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultado en Llansantffraed, Monmouthshire, Gales.
